question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R7 changes the IS-IS circuit-type on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (R7↔R6) to
`level-1`. R6 stays at L2 (inherited from `is-type level-2-only`). The
two ends speak different IIH levels; no adjacency forms. The lower-
core path R2-R6-R7-R4 is partitioned; traffic from R6 to R7 must
detour through R2-R3-R4-R7.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.107 (R7), the IS-IS circuit-type on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R7<->R6 link) was set to level-1 while R6 operates as level-2-only (inherited from is-type).
The two ends exchange IIH hellos at different levels, so they never agree on a common level and the IS-IS adjacency cannot form on that link. The lower-core path R2-R6-R7-R4 is partitioned and R6<->R7 traffic must detour via R2-R3-R4-R7.
Fix: remove the circuit-type override on R7's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (return it to level-2).
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply — silent scenario. The circuit-
type mismatch silently prevents the adjacency from re-forming on
the next hello cycle; no ADJCHANGE Down log is emitted because the
adjacency had not yet fully established (or the previous-state log
scrolls off before scenario-verify post-snapshot). Diagnostic ground
truth lives in expected_state_diffs.
expected_state_diffs: R7 `show running-config router isis LAB interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0`: `circuit-type level-1` line present (baseline: absent — interface inherits global L2-only).
R7 `show isis interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 | inc Level|Circuit`: Circuit Type = Level-1 (other interfaces on R7 still report Level-2).
R7 `show isis adjacency`: R6 no longer listed under Gi0/0/0/0 (baseline: R6 was a Level-2 adjacency on this link).
R6 `show isis adjacency`: R7 missing on the corresponding Gi interface — mismatched IIH levels prevent the adjacency from forming.
R7 `show route 192.0.2.6`: path detours via R4 → R3 → R2 → R6 instead of direct R7-R6 (longer label stack visible in `show mpls forwarding`).

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 circuit-type level-1

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 no circuit-type
